description_long: This object is a potted flowering plant featuring several tall, slender green stems with elongated, lanceolate leaves arranged alternately along the stems. At the top, there are multiple large, showy orange flowers with prominent petals radiating outward in a star-like, radial pattern, each flower displaying visible stamens and pistils at the center. The petals are slightly curved and overlap at the base, giving the blooms a layered appearance. The plant is housed in a dark, rounded pot with a subtle lip and sits on a matching saucer, both of which appear to be made of ceramic or clay. The overall look is naturalistic, with the flowers and foliage providing a vibrant contrast to the muted, earthy tones of the pot. This type of potted flower is commonly found in urban settings such as sidewalks, parks, patios, or building entrances, serving as decorative greenery.
description: A potted plant with tall green stems, lanceolate leaves, and large orange star-shaped flowers in a dark ceramic pot. The flowers have prominent, radiating petals and visible stamens.
description_view_0: Front view showing two fully open orange flowers and dense green foliage in a dark pot.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a single prominent flower and the elongated leaves, with the pot's rounded shape visible.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying both flowers and the layered arrangement of petals, with the pot and saucer clearly seen.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the flowers from a different angle, with one flower partially obscured and the foliage more prominent.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, star-like pattern with overlapping bases, forming a whorled configuration typical of many ornamental lilies. The arrangement is symmetrical and the petals are slightly curved outward.
